=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n481\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n482\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n486\t//\n487\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n488\t//\n489\t// Example usage:\n490\t//\n491\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n492\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n493\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n494\t//\n495\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n496\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n497\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n498\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n499\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n500\t\t\treturn \"\"\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n504\t\n505\t\t// Format expected value\n506\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n507\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n508\t\t\tcase int:\n509\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n510\t\t\tcase []int:\n511\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n512\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n513\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n516\t\t\tcase string:\n517\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n518\t\t\tcase float64:\n519\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n520\t\t\tcase []string:\n521\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n522\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n523\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n526\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n527\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n528\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n529\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n530\t\t\tdefault:\n531\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Format actual value\n536\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n537\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n538\t\t\tcase int:\n539\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n540\t\t\tcase string:\n541\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n542\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n543\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n546\t\t\tcase float64:\n547\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n548\t\t\tcase []string:\n549\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n550\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n551\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n552\t\t\t\t}\n553\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n554\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n555\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n556\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n557\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n558\t\t\tdefault:\n559\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Build the formatted string\n564\t\tvar parts []string\n565\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n566\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n567\t\t}\n568\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n569\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n573\t\t\treturn \"\"\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n580\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n581\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n582\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\tvar pairs []string\n586\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n587\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n591\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n592\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n593\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n600\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n601\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n602\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tvar items []string\n606\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n607\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n611\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n612\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n613\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n620\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n621\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n622\t//\n623\t// Parameters:\n624\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n625\t//\n626\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n627\t//\n628\t// Example usage:\n629\t//\n630\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n631\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n633\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n634\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n635\t\t\treturn \"\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n643\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn \"\"\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n658\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n659\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n660\t//\n661\t// Parameters:\n662\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n663\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n664\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n665\t//\n666\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n667\t//\n668\t// Example usage:\n669\t//\n670\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n671\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n672\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n673\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n674\t//\t}\n675\t//\n676\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n677\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n678\t//\n679\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n680\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n681\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n682\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n683\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n684\t\n685\t\t// Add severity if requested\n686\t\tif includeSeverity {\n687\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n688\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n689\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n690\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n691\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n692\t\t\t} else {\n693\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n694\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n695\t\t\t}\n696\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Add error type and field\n700\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n701\t\n702\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n703\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n704\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n705\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n706\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n712\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n713\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n718\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n719\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n723\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n724\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n725\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n728\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n732\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n733\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n737\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n741\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n743\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n744\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n745\t\t\t}\n746\t\t}\n747\t\n748\t\treturn builder.String()\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n752\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n753\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n757\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n758\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n759\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n760\t//\n761\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n762\t//\n763\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n764\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n765\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n770\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n771\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n772\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\n775\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n776\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n777\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n778\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n779\t//\n780\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n781\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n782\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n783\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n784\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n785\t\n786\t\t// Add severity if requested\n787\t\tif includeSeverity {\n788\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n789\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n790\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n791\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n792\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n793\t\t\t} else {\n794\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n795\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n796\t\t\t}\n797\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n798\t\t}\n799\t\n800\t\t// Add error type and field\n801\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n802\t\n803\t\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n804\t\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n805\t\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n806\t\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n807\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n810\t\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n811\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n812\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n813\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n814\t\t\t}\n815\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n816\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n817\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n819\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n820\t\t\t}\n821\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n822\t\t}\n823\t\n824\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n825\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n826\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n827\t\t}\n828\t\n829\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n830\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n831\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n832\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n833\t\n834\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n835\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n836\t\t\t}\n837\t\n838\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n839\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n840\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n841\t\t\t}\n842\t\n843\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n844\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n848\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n850\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n851\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n852\t\t\t}\n853\t\t}\n854\t\n855\t\treturn builder.String()\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n859\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n860\t//\n861\t// Parameters:\n862\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n863\t//\n864\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n865\t//\n866\t// Example usage:\n867\t//\n868\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n869\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n870\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n871\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n872\t//\t}\n873\t//\n874\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n875\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n876\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n877\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n878\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n879\t\t}\n880\t\n881\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n882\t}\n883\t\n884\t// =============================================================================\n885\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n886\t// =============================================================================\n887\t\n888\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n889\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n890\t//\n891\t// Parameters:\n892\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n893\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n894\t//\n895\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n896\t//\n897\t// Example usage:\n898\t//\n899\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n900\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n901\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n902\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n903\t//\t}, {\n904\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n905\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n906\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n907\t//\t}}\n908\t//\n909\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n910\t//\t// Returns:\n911\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n912\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n913\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n914\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n915\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n916\t\t}\n917\t\n918\t\tvar lines []string\n919\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n920\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n921\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n922\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n923\t\t}\n924\t\n925\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n926\t}\n927\t\n928\t// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n929\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n930\t//\n931\t// Parameters:\n932\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n933\t//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n934\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n935\t//\n936\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n937\t//\n938\t// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n939\t//\n940\t// Example usage:\n941\t//\n942\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n943\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n944\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n945\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n946\t//\t}, {\n947\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n948\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n949\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n950\t//\t}}\n951\t//\n952\t//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n953\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n954\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n955\t//\t}\n956\t//\n957\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n958\t//\t// Returns:\n959\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n960\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\n961\tfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n962\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n963\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\tvar lines []string\n967\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n968\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n969\t\n970\t\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n971\t\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n972\t\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n973\t\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n976\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n977\t\t}\n978\t\n979\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n980\t}\n981\t\n982\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n983\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n984\t//\n985\t// Parameters:\n986\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n987\t//\n988\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n989\t//\n990\t// Example usage:\n991\t//\n992\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n993\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n994\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n995\t//\t}, {\n996\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n997\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n998\t//\t}}\n999\t//\n1000\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n1001\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n1002\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n1003\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1004\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1005\t\t}\n1006\t\n1007\t\t// Count errors by type\n1008\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n1009\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n1010\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n1011\t\t}\n1012\t\n1013\t\t// Build summary parts\n1014\t\tvar parts []string\n1015\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n1016\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// =============================================================================\n1023\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n1024\t// =============================================================================\n1025\t\n1026\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n1027\t// without the parent parameter.\n1028\t//\n1029\t// Parameters:\n1030\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n1031\t//   - location: Additional location context\n1032\t//\n1033\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n1034\t//\n1035\t// Example usage:\n1036\t//\n1037\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n1038\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n1039\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n1040\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n1041\t}\n1042\t\n1043\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n1044\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n1045\t//\n1046\t// Parameters:\n1047\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n1048\t//\n1049\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n1050\t//\n1051\t// Example usage:\n1052\t//\n1053\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n1054\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n1055\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n1056\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n1057\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n1060\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n1061\t//\n1062\t// Parameters:\n1063\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1064\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1065\t//\n1066\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1067\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n1068\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n1069\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n1072\t// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n1073\t// of formatting an error with context.\n1074\t//\n1075\t// Parameters:\n1076\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1077\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1078\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n1079\t//\n1080\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1081\t//\n1082\t// Example usage:\n1083\t//\n1084\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n1085\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n1086\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n1087\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n1088\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n1089\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n1092\t//\n1093\t// Parameters:\n1094\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n1095\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1096\t//\n1097\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n1098\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1099\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n1100\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t// =============================================================================\n1103\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1104\t// =============================================================================\n1105\t\n1106\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n1107\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n1108\t//\n1109\t// Parameters:\n1110\t//   - value: The value to quote\n1111\t//\n1112\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n1113\t//\n1114\t// Example usage:\n1115\t//\n1116\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n1117\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n1118\t//\n1119\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n1120\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n1121\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n1122\t\tif value == \"\" {\n1123\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n1124\t\t}\n1125\t\n1126\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n1127\t}\n1128\t\n1129\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n1130\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n1131\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n1132\t//\n1133\t// Parameters:\n1134\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n1135\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1136\t//\n1137\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n1138\t//\n1139\t// Example usage:\n1140\t//\n1141\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n1142\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n1143\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1144\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n1145\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n1146\t\t}\n1147\t\n1148\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n1149\t\t\treturn value\n1150\t\t}\n1151\t\n1152\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n1153\t}\n1154\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_071c806965504d06ab01fa0e","session_id":"1fd33d17-46f1-410a-9c65-c0e88d1c3c4a","uuid":"7c9ac347-f79b-4e33-a3ff-42f8a0a643b1","timestamp":"2026-07-15T21:47:33.395Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore validation code structure"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a27b575a3baf459daaab882d","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Validation Error Formatting Data Structure Design\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document defines the data structure for consistent validation error formatting in the ARMOR validation library. The design provides a structured approach to representing validation failures with rich context, actionable suggestions, and clear expected vs actual value comparisons.\n6\t\n7\t## Core Design Principles\n8\t\n9\t1. **Consistency**: All validation errors follow the same structural pattern\n10\t2. **Actionability**: Errors include suggestions for resolution\n11\t3. **Context**: Errors include sufficient context to understand the validation failure\n12\t4. **Flexibility**: The structure supports various validation types while maintaining consistency\n13\t5. **Composability**: Builders and helpers make error creation ergonomic\n14\t\n15\t## Primary Data Structure: ValidationError\n16\t\n17\tThe `ValidationError` struct is the core type representing a validation failure.\n18\t\n19\t```go\n20\ttype ValidationError struct {\n21\t    // REQUIRED FIELDS\n22\t    \n23\t    // ValidationType indicates the category of validation being performed.\n24\t    // Common values: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\", \"status_code_range\"\n25\t    // This field is REQUIRED and must be non-empty.\n26\t    ValidationType string\n27\t    \n28\t    // Expected represents the value or condition that was expected.\n29\t    // Type can be: int, []int, string, or other comparable types\n30\t    // This field is REQUIRED.\n31\t    Expected interface{}\n32\t    \n33\t    // Actual represents the value or condition that was actually received.\n34\t    // Type should match Expected for meaningful comparison\n35\t    // This field is REQUIRED.\n36\t    Actual interface{}\n37\t    \n38\t    // OPTIONAL FIELDS - Contextual Information\n39\t    \n40\t    // Context provides additional information about the validation operation.\n41\t    // Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n42\t    // This field is OPTIONAL but recommended for debugging.\n43\t    Context string\n44\t    \n45\t    // FieldName specifies the field where the error was found.\n46\t    // Primarily used for error message validation (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n47\t    // This field is OPTIONAL.\n48\t    FieldName string\n49\t    \n50\t    // ResponseSnippet contains a truncated excerpt from the response body.\n51\t    // Limited to ~200 characters for readability\n52\t    // This field is OPTIONAL but recommended for debugging.\n53\t    ResponseSnippet string\n54\t    \n55\t    // OPTIONAL FIELDS - Validation-Specific Details\n56\t    \n57\t    // PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n58\t    // Used when validating against regex patterns (e.g., \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n59\t    // This field is OPTIONAL.\n60\t    PatternDetails string\n61\t    \n62\t    // RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n63\t    // Format: \"400-499 (Client Error)\" or similar\n64\t    // This field is OPTIONAL.\n65\t    RangeInfo string\n66\t    \n67\t    // ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n68\t    // Each string provides a piece of granular detail about what was checked and what failed.\n69\t    // This field is OPTIONAL.\n70\t    ValidationDetails []string\n71\t    \n72\t    // OPTIONAL FIELDS - Resolution Guidance\n73\t    \n74\t    // Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for fixing the validation failure.\n75\t    // If nil or empty, suggestions are auto-generated based on validation type and values.\n76\t    // This field is OPTIONAL (auto-generated when not provided).\n77\t    Suggestions []string\n78\t}\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Field Classification Summary\n82\t\n83\t| Field | Required | Purpose |\n84\t|-------|----------|---------|\n85\t| `ValidationType` | **Yes** | Identifies the validation category |\n86\t| `Expected` | **Yes** | What was expected |\n87\t| `Actual` | **Yes** | What was actually received |\n88\t| `Context` | No | Additional operation context |\n89\t| `FieldName` | No | Specific field name (message validation) |\n90\t| `ResponseSnippet` | No | Debugging excerpt from response |\n91\t| `PatternDetails` | No | Pattern matching information |\n92\t| `RangeInfo` | No | Range boundary information |\n93\t| `ValidationDetails` | No | Additional validation-specific details |\n94\t| `Suggestions` | No | Resolution recommendations (auto-generated if empty) |\n95\t\n96\t## Supporting Types\n97\t\n98\t### StatusCodeValidationResult\n99\t\n100\tDetailed result for status code validation operations.\n101\t\n102\t```go\n103\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n104\t    // Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n105\t    Valid bool\n106\t    \n107\t    // ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n108\t    ActualCode int\n109\t    \n110\t    // ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s)\n111\t    ExpectedCodes []int\n112\t    \n113\t    // MatchedCode is the specific code that matched (if any)\n114\t    MatchedCode *int\n115\t    \n116\t    // MismatchDetails contains human-readable mismatch information\n117\t    MismatchDetails string\n118\t    \n119\t    // IsClientError indicates if actual code is 4xx\n120\t    IsClientError bool\n121\t    \n122\t    // IsServerError indicates if actual code is 5xx\n123\t    IsServerError bool\n124\t    \n125\t    // Category describes the general category: \"success\", \"client_error\", \"server_error\", \"redirection\", \"other\"\n126\t    Category string\n127\t}\n128\t```\n129\t\n130\t### ErrorMessageValidationResult\n131\t\n132\tDetailed result for error message content validation.\n133\t\n134\t```go\n135\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n136\t    // Valid indicates whether the error message validation passed\n137\t    Valid bool\n138\t    \n139\t    // Found indicates whether an error message field was found\n140\t    Found bool\n141\t    \n142\t    // Message is the actual error message content\n143\t    Message string\n144\t    \n145\t    // FieldName is the field where the message was found\n146\t    FieldName string\n147\t    \n148\t    // PatternMatched indicates whether the regex pattern matched\n149\t    PatternMatched bool\n150\t    \n151\t    // MustContainResults shows which required strings were found\n152\t    MustContainResults map[string]bool\n153\t    \n154\t    // MustNotContainResults shows which forbidden strings were found\n155\t    MustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n156\t    \n157\t    // LengthValidation indicates whether message length was valid\n158\t    LengthValidation bool\n159\t    \n160\t    // Issues contains a list of validation issues\n161\t    Issues []string\n162\t}\n163\t```\n164\t\n165\t### ErrorCodeMatch\n166\t\n167\tRepresents a found error code in a response.\n168\t\n169\t```go\n170\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n171\t    // FieldName is the field where the error code was found\n172\t    FieldName string\n173\t    \n174\t    // CodeValue is the error code value (as string for flexibility)\n175\t    CodeValue string\n176\t    \n177\t    // NumericCode is the code parsed as integer (if applicable)\n178\t    NumericCode *int\n179\t    \n180\t    // MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched\n181\t    MatchedPattern string\n182\t    \n183\t    // Position describes where in the response the code was found\n184\t    Position string\n185\t}\n186\t```\n187\t\n188\t## Builder Pattern: ValidationFormatter\n189\t\n190\tThe `ValidationFormatter` provides a fluent builder API for constructing ValidationError instances.\n191\t\n192\t```go\n193\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n194\t    validationType     string\n195\t    expected           interface{}\n196\t    actual             interface{}\n197\t    context            string\n198\t    responseSnippet    string\n199\t    fieldName          string\n200\t    patternDetails     string\n201\t    rangeInfo          string\n202\t    validationDetails  []string\n203\t    customSuggestions  []string\n204\t}\n205\t```\n206\t\n207\t### Builder Methods\n208\t\n209\t- `NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter`\n210\t- `WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter`\n211\t- `WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter`\n212\t- `WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter`\n213\t- `WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter`\n214\t- `WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter`\n215\t- `WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter`\n216\t- `WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter`\n217\t- `WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter`\n218\t- `WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter`\n219\t- `Format() ValidationError`\n220\t\n221\t### Example Usage\n222\t\n223\t```go\n224\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n225\t    WithExpected(200).\n226\t    WithActual(404).\n227\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n228\t    WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"User not found\"}`).\n229\t    Format()\n230\t```\n231\t\n232\t## Convenience Functions\n233\t\n234\tFor common validation scenarios, dedicated functions provide a more ergonomic API:\n235\t\n236\t### Status Code Validation\n237\t\n238\t```go\n239\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError\n240\t```\n241\t\n242\t### Error Message Validation\n243\t\n244\t```go\n245\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError\n246\t```\n247\t\n248\t### Status Code Range Validation\n249\t\n250\t```go\n251\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError\n252\t```\n253\t\n254\t### Content-Type Validation\n255\t\n256\t```go\n257\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError\n258\t```\n259\t\n260\t### Custom Validation\n261\t\n262\t```go\n263\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n264\t    validationType string,\n265\t    expected, actual interface{},\n266\t    options ...FormatOption,\n267\t) ValidationError\n268\t```\n269\t\n270\t## Format Options Pattern\n271\t\n272\tFor maximum flexibility, the `FormatOption` pattern allows optional configuration:\n273\t\n274\t```go\n275\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n276\t\n277\t// Available options:\n278\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption\n279\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption\n280\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption\n281\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption\n282\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption\n283\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption\n284\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption\n285\t```\n286\t\n287\t### Example Usage\n288\t\n289\t```go\n290\terr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n291\t    \"custom_field\",\n292\t    \"required_value\",\n293\t    \"actual_value\",\n294\t    WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n295\t    WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n296\t    WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n297\t)\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t## Error Formatting Output\n301\t\n302\tThe `ValidationError.Error()` method formats the error as a structured multi-line message:\n303\t\n304\t```\n305\t{validation_type} validation failed\n306\t  Expected: {expected_value}\n307\t  Actual:   {actual_value}\n308\t  Context:  {context}           // if provided\n309\t  Field:    {field_name}        // if provided\n310\t  Pattern:  {pattern_details}   // if provided\n311\t  Range:    {range_info}        // if provided\n312\t  Response: {response_snippet}  // if provided\n313\t  Details:                      // if provided\n314\t    - {detail_1}\n315\t    - {detail_2}\n316\t  Suggestions:\n317\t    - {suggestion_1}\n318\t    - {suggestion_2}\n319\t```\n320\t\n321\t## Design Decisions\n322\t\n323\t### 1. Interface{} for Expected/Actual Values\n324\t\n325\t**Decision**: Use `interface{}` for Expected and Actual fields.\n326\t\n327\t**Rationale**: \n328\t- Flexibility to handle different validation types (int codes, string patterns, ranges)\n329\t- Type safety is maintained through validation functions\n330\t- Allows for single struct definition across all validation types\n331\t\n332\t**Trade-off**: Requires type assertions in consumer code, but this is acceptable for a validation library.\n333\t\n334\t### 2. Auto-Generated Suggestions\n335\t\n336\t**Decision**: Suggestions are auto-generated when not explicitly provided.\n337\t\n338\t**Rationale**:\n339\t- Reduces boilerplate in common validation scenarios\n340\t- Ensures consistent quality of suggestions\n341\t- Allows override for domain-specific cases\n342\t\n343\t**Implementation**: The `generateSuggestions()` function analyzes validation type and values to produce relevant suggestions.\n344\t\n345\t### 3. Optional Context Fields\n346\t\n347\t**Decision**: Context, ResponseSnippet, and field-specific details are optional.\n348\t\n349\t**Rationale**:\n350\t- Not all validation scenarios have access to full response data\n351\t- Allows for lightweight error creation when context is unavailable\n352\t- Maintains consistency across all error types\n353\t\n354\t### 4. Structured Error Format\n355\t\n356\t**Decision**: Multi-line structured format with clear sections.\n357\t\n358\t**Rationale**:\n359\t- Readable in logs and console output\n360\t- Machine-parsable if needed\n361\t- Follows common error message patterns\n362\t\n363\t### 5. Builder Pattern\n364\t\n365\t**Decision**: Provide fluent builder API alongside direct struct construction.\n366\t\n367\t**Rationale**:\n368\t- Ergonomic for common cases\n369\t- Flexible for advanced scenarios\n370\t- Allows progressive construction of complex errors\n371\t\n372\t## Validation Type Categories\n373\t\n374\tThe following validation types are currently supported:\n375\t\n376\t| Type | Description | Expected Value Type | Actual Value Type |\n377\t|------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------|\n378\t| `status_code` | HTTP status code validation | int or []int | int |\n379\t| `error_message` | Error message content validation | string (pattern) | string (message) |\n380\t| `content_type` | Content-Type header validation | string | string |\n381\t| `status_code_range` | Status code range validation | string (pattern) | int |\n382\t\n383\t## Extensibility\n384\t\n385\t### Adding New Validation Types\n386\t\n387\tTo add a new validation type:\n388\t\n389\t1. Define the type name (e.g., `\"header_validation\"`)\n390\t2. Add type-specific suggestion generation in `generateSuggestions()`\n391\t3. Optionally create a convenience function `Format{Type}Error()`\n392\t4. Update documentation\n393\t\n394\t### Custom Suggestions\n395\t\n396\tFor domain-specific validation scenarios:\n397\t\n398\t```go\n399\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_validation\").\n400\t    WithExpected(expected).\n401\t    WithActual(actual).\n402\t    WithSuggestions(\n403\t        \"Check the configuration file\",\n404\t        \"Verify the service is running\",\n405\t        \"Review the logs for details\",\n406\t    ).\n407\t    Format()\n408\t```\n409\t\n410\t## Summary\n411\t\n412\tThe validation error formatting data structure provides:\n413\t\n414\t1. **Consistency**: Single `ValidationError` type for all validation failures\n415\t2. **Rich Context**: Optional fields provide debugging information\n416\t3. **Actionability**: Auto-generated or custom suggestions guide resolution\n417\t4. **Ergonomics**: Builder pattern and convenience functions simplify usage\n418\t5. **Extensibility**: Design supports new validation types and custom scenarios\n419\t\n420\tThe structure balances flexibility and consistency, making it suitable for a wide range of validation scenarios while maintaining a clear, predictable error format."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_071c806965504d06ab01fa0e","session_id":"1fd33d17-46f1-410a-9c65-c0e88d1c3c4a","uuid":"b7ce9f05-f7ab-436c-8aac-8911d450f748","timestamp":"2026-07-15T21:47:41.742Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore validation code structure"}

expected type.\n76\t\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n77\t\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n78\t\n79\t\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific Review the error message for specific details\n108\t\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n109\t\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// Example 6: Status code range validation\n113\tfunc ExampleValidationError_statusCodeRange() {\n114\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n115\t\n116\t\tfmt.Println(err.Error())\n117\t\t// Output:\n118\t\t// status_code_range validation failed\n119\t\t//   Expected: 4xx (Client Error)\n120\t\t//   Actual:   200\n121\t\t//   Context:  error response check\n122\t\t//   Range:    400-499 (Client Error)\n123\t\t//   Details:\n124\t\t//     - Status code 200 is outside range 400-499\n125\t\t//   Suggestions:\n126\t\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n127\t\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n128\t\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\t// Example 7: Content-Type validation\n132\tfunc ExampleValidationError_contentType() {\n133\t\terr := FormatContentTypeError(\n134\t\t\t\"application/json\",\n135\t\t\t\"text/html\",\n136\t\t\t\"API response\",\n137\t\t)\n138\t\n139\t\tfmt.Println(err.Error())\n140\t\t// Output:\n141\t\t// content_type validation failed\n142\t\t//   Expected: application/json\n143\t\t//   Actual:   text/html\n144\t\t//   Context:  API response\n145\t\t//   Suggestions:\n146\t\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n147\t\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n148\t\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// Example 8: Custom validation with format options\n152\tfunc ExampleValidationError_customWithOptions() {\n153\t\terr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n154\t\t\t\"header_validation\",\n155\t\t\t\"Bearer Token\",\n156\t\t\t\"\",\n157\t\t\tWithContext(\"Authorization header check\"),\n158\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(`{\"headers\": {\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}}`),\n159\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n160\t\t\t\t\"Authorization header is missing\",\n161\t\t\t\t\"Expected 'Bearer' token format\",\n162\t\t\t),\n163\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n164\t\t\t\t\"Add Authorization header with Bearer token\",\n165\t\t\t\t\"Verify token is properly formatted\",\n166\t\t\t\t\"Check if authentication is required for this endpoint\",\n167\t\t\t),\n168\t\t)\n169\t\n170\t\tfmt.Println(err.Error())\n171\t\t// Output:\n172\t\t// header_validation validation failed\n173\t\t//   Expected: Bearer Token\n174\t\t//   Actual:\n175\t\t//   Context:  Authorization header check\n176\t\t//   Response: {\"headers\": {\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}}\n177\t\t//   Details:\n178\t\t//     - Check if authentication is required for this endpoint\n184\t}\n185\t\n186\t// Example 9: Custom validation with pattern details\n187\tfunc ExampleValidationError_withPatternDetails() {\n188\t\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n189\t\t\tWithExpected(\"authentication.*failed\").\n190\t\t\tWithActual(\"access denied\").\n191\t\t\tWithFieldName(\"error\").\n192\t\t\tWithContext(\"Authentication check\").\n193\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'authentication.*failed' did not match\").\n194\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n195\t\t\t\t\"Searched for regex pattern 'authentication.*failed' in field 'error'\",\n196\t\t\t\t\"Actual message: \\\"access denied\\\"\",\n197\t\t\t).\n198\t\t\tFormat()\n199\t\n200\t\tfmt.Println(err.Error())\n201\t\t// Output:\n202\t\t// error_message validation failed\n203\t\t//   Expected: authentication.*failed\n204\t\t//   Actual:   access denied\n205\t\t//   Context:  Authentication check\n206\t\t//   Field:    error\n207\t\t//   Pattern:  regex pattern 'authentication.*failed' did not match\n208\t\t//   Details:\n209\t\t//     -
