validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1004\t//\t)\n1005\tfunc WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n1006\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1007\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n1008\t\t}\n1009\t}\n1010\t\n1011\t// WithStatusCode creates a FormatOption that sets the HTTP status code for HTTP errors.\n1012\t// Use this with HTTP-related errors to include status code information in the formatted message.\n1013\t//\n1014\t// Example usage:\n1015\t//\n1016\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1017\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1018\t//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n1019\t//\t    \"endpoint\",\n1020\t//\t    validate.WithStatusCode(404),\n1021\t//\t)\n1022\tfunc WithStatusCode(statusCode int) FormatOption {\n1023\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1024\t\t\tc.StatusCode = statusCode\n1025\t\t}\n1026\t}\n1027\t\n1028\t// WithTimeout creates a FormatOption that sets the timeout for performance errors.\n1029\t// Use this with performance-related errors to include timeout information in the formatted message.\n1030\t//\n1031\t// Example usage:\n1032\t//\n1033\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1034\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1035\t//\t    \"Request timed out\",\n1036\t//\t    \"api_response\",\n1037\t//\t    validate.WithTimeout(5000),\n1038\t//\t)\n1039\tfunc WithTimeout(timeout int) FormatOption {\n1040\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1041\t\t\tc.Timeout = timeout\n1042\t\t}\n1043\t}\n1044\t\n1045\t// WithSecurityContext creates a FormatOption that sets the security context for security errors.\n1046\t// Use this with security-related errors to include security context in the formatted message.\n1047\t//\n1048\t// Example usage:\n1049\t//\n1050\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1051\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1052\t//\t    \"Invalid credentials\",\n1053\t//\t    \"Authorization\",\n1054\t//\t    validate.WithSecurityContext(\"authentication\"),\n1055\t//\t)\n1056\tfunc WithSecurityContext(securityContext string) FormatOption {\n1057\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1058\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n1059\t\t}\n1060\t}\n1061\t\n1062\t\n1063\t// =============================================================================\n1064\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1065\t// =============================================================================\n1066\t\n1067\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1068\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1069\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1070\t\tif err != nil {\n1071\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1072\t\t}\n1073\t\n1074\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1075\t\tif err != nil {\n1076\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1077\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1078\t\t}\n1079\t\n1080\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1081\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t// =============================================================================\n1084\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1085\t// =============================================================================\n1086\t\n1087\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1088\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1089\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1090\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1091\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1092\t}\n1093\t\n1094\t// global tracker instance\n1095\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1096\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1100\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1101\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// Parameters:\n1104\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1105\t//\n1106\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1107\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1108\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1109\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1110\t\n1111\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1112\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1113\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1116\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1117\t// configuration issues.\n1118\t//\n1119\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1120\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1121\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1122\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1123\t\n1124\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1125\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1126\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1127\t\t}\n1128\t\treturn result\n1129\t}\n1130\t\n1131\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1132\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1133\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1134\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1135\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1136\t\n1137\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1138\t}\n1139\t\n1140\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1141\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1142\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1143\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1144\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1145\t\n1146\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1147\t}\n1148\t\n1149\t// =============================================================================\n1150\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1151\t// =============================================================================\n1152\t\n1153\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1154\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1155\t//\n1156\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1157\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1158\t//\n1159\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1160\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1161\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1162\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1163\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1164\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1165\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1166\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1167\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1168\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1169\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1170\t//\n1171\t// Parameters:\n1172\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1173\t//   - message: The error message content\n1174\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1175\t//\n1176\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1177\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1178\t//\n1179\t// Example usage:\n1180\t//\n1181\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1182\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1183\t//\n1184\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1185\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1186\t//\n1187\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1188\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1189\t//\n1190\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1191\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1192\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1193\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1194\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1195\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1196\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1197\t\n1198\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1199\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1200\t\n1201\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1202\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1203\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1204\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1205\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1206\t\t\t} else {\n1207\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1208\t\t\t}\n1209\t\t}\n1210\t\n1211\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1212\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1213\t}\n1214\t\n1215\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1216\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1217\t//\n1218\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1219\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1220\t//\n1221\t// Error Type Validation:\n1222\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1223\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1224\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1225\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1226\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1227\t//\n1228\t// Parameters:\n1229\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1230\t//   - message: The error message content\n1231\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1232\t//\n1233\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1234\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1235\t//\n1236\t// Example usage:\n1237\t//\n1238\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1239\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1240\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1241\t//\n1242\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1243\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1244\t//\n1245\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1246\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1247\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1248\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1249\t//\n1250\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1251\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1252\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1253\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1254\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1255\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1256\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1257\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1258\t\t}\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1261\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1262\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1263\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1264\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1265\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1266\t\n1267\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1268\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1269\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1270\t\t}\n1271\t\n1272\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1273\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1274\t\n1275\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1276\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1277\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1278\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1279\t\n1280\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1281\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1282\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1283\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1284\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1285\t\t}\n1286\t\n1287\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1288\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1289\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1290\t\n1291\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1292\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1293\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1294\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1295\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1296\t\t\t} else {\n1297\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1298\t\t\t}\n1299\t\t}\n1300\t\n1301\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1302\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1303\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1304\t\t}\n1305\t\n1306\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1307\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1308\t}\n1309\t\n1310\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1311\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1312\t//\n1313\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1314\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1315\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1316\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1317\t}\n1318\t\n1319\t// =============================================================================\n1320\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1321\t// =============================================================================\n1322\t\n1323\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1324\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1325\t\n1326\tconst (\n1327\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1328\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1329\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1330\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1331\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1332\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1333\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1334\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1335\t)\n1336\t\n1337\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1338\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1339\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1340\t\tprefix     string\n1341\t}\n1342\t\n1343\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1344\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1345\t\n1346\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1347\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1348\t//\n1349\t// Example usage:\n1350\t//\n1351\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1352\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1353\t//\n1354\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1355\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1356\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1357\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1358\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1359\t\t}\n1360\t}\n1361\t\n1362\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1363\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1364\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1365\t//\n1366\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1367\t//\n1368\t// Example usage:\n1369\t//\n1370\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1371\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1372\t//\n1373\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1374\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1375\t//\n1376\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1377\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1378\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1379\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1380\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t}\n1383\t\n1384\t// =============================================================================\n1385\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1386\t// =============================================================================\n1387\t\n1388\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1389\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1390\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1391\t//\n1392\t// Parameters:\n1393\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1394\t//\n1395\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1396\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1397\t//\n1398\t// Example usage:\n1399\t//\n1400\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1401\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1402\t//\n1403\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1404\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1405\t//\n1406\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1407\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1408\t//\n1409\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1410\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1411\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1412\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1413\t\n1414\t\t// Handle empty path\n1415\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1416\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1417\t\t}\n1418\t\n1419\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1420\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1421\t}\n1422\t\n1423\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1424\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1425\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1426\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1427\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1428\t\t}\n1429\t\n1430\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1431\t\tvar result []string\n1432\t\n1433\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1434\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1435\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1436\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1437\t\t\t}\n1438\t\n1439\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1440\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1441\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1442\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1443\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1444\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1445\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1446\t\t\t\t} else {\n1447\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1448\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1449\t\t\t\t}\n1450\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1451\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1452\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1453\t\t\t} else {\n1454\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1455\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1456\t\t\t}\n1457\t\t}\n1458\t\n1459\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1460\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1461\t\t}\n1462\t\n1463\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1464\t}\n1465\t\n1466\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1467\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1468\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1469\t\t\treturn false\n1470\t\t}\n1471\t\n1472\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1473\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1474\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1475\t\t\t\treturn false\n1476\t\t\t}\n1477\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1478\t\t}\n1479\t\n1480\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1481\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1482\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1483\t\t\t\treturn false\n1484\t\t\t}\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\n1487\t\treturn true\n1488\t}\n1489\t\n1490\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1491\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1492\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1493\t}\n1494\t\n1495\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1496\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1497\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1498\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1499\t//\n1500\t// Parameters:\n1501\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1502\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1503\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1504\t//\n1505\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1506\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1507\t//\n1508\t// Example usage:\n1509\t//\n1510\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1511\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1512\t//\n1513\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1514\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1515\t//\n1516\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1517\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1518\t//\n1519\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1520\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1521\t//\n1522\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1523\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1524\t//\n1525\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1526\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1527\t//\n1528\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1529\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1530\t//\n1531\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1532\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1533\t//\n1534\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1535\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1536\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1537\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1538\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1539\t\n1540\t\t// Apply options\n1541\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1542\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1543\t\t\topt(config)\n1544\t\t}\n1545\t\n1546\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1547\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1548\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1549\t\t}\n1550\t\n1551\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1552\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1553\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1554\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1555\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1556\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1557\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1558\t\t\t\t}\n1559\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1560\t\t\t}\n1561\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1562\t\t}\n1563\t\n1564\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1565\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1566\t\n1567\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1568\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1569\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1570\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1571\t\t\t}\n1572\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1573\t\t}\n1574\t\n1575\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1576\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1577\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1581\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1582\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1583\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1584\t\n1585\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1586\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1587\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1588\t\t\t}\n1589\t\n1590\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1591\t\t}\n1592\t\n1593\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1594\t}\n1595\t\n1596\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1597\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1598\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1599\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1600\t\t\treturn path\n1601\t\t}\n1602\t\n1603\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1604\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1605\t\tinBrackets := false\n1606\t\n1607\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1608\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1609\t\n1610\t\t\tswitch {\n1611\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1612\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1613\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1614\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1615\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1616\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1617\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1618\t\t\t\t}\n1619\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1620\t\n1621\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1622\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1623\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1624\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1625\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1626\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1627\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1628\t\t\t\t}\n1629\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1630\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1631\t\n1632\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1633\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1634\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1635\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1636\t\n1637\t\t\tdefault:\n1638\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1639\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1640\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1641\t\t\t\t} else {\n1642\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1643\t\t\t\t}\n1644\t\t\t}\n1645\t\t}\n1646\t\n1647\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1648\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1649\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1650\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1651\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1652\t\t}\n1653\t\n1654\t\treturn result.String()\n1655\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t// =============================================================================\n1658\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1659\t// =============================================================================\n1660\t\n1661\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1662\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1663\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1664\t//\n1665\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1666\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1667\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1668\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1669\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1670\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1671\t//\n1672\t// Parameters:\n1673\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1674\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1675\t//   - message: The error message content\n1676\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1677\t//\n1678\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1679\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1680\t//\n1681\t// Example usage:\n1682\t//\n1683\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1684\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1685\t//\n1686\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1687\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1688\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1689\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1690\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1691\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1692\t\t}\n1693\t\n1694\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1695\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1696\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1697\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1698\t\n1699\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1700\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1701\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1702\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1703\t\t\t} else {\n1704\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1705\t\t\t}\n1706\t\t}\n1707\t\n1708\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1709\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1710\t\n1711\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1712\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1713\t\n1714\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1715\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1716\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1717\t\n1718\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1719\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1720\t\n1721\t\t// Add field name if present\n1722\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1723\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1724\t\t}\n1725\t\n1726\t\t// Add message\n1727\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1728\t\n1729\t\treturn builder.String()\n1730\t}\n1731\t\n1732\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1733\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1734\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1735\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1736\t\n1737\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1738\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1739\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1740\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1741\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1742\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\treturn \"\"\n1746\t}\n1747\t\n1748\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1749\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1750\t\tswitch severity {\n1751\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1752\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1753\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1754\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1755\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1756\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1757\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1758\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1759\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1760\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1761\t\tdefault:\n1762\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1763\t\t}\n1764\t}\n1765\t\n1766\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1767\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1768\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1769\t//\n1770\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1771\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1772\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1773\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1774\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1775\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1776\t//\n1777\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1778\t//\n1779\t// Parameters:\n1780\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1781\t//   - message: The error message content\n1782\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1783\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1784\t//\n1785\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1786\t//\n1787\t// Example usage:\n1788\t//\n1789\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1790\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1791\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1792\t//\t    \"email\",\n1793\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1794\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1795\t//\t)\n1796\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1797\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1798\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1799\t\tmessage string,\n1800\t\tfieldName string,\n1801\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1802\t) string {\n1803\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1804\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1805\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1806\t\n1807\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1808\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1809\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1810\t\t\topt(config)\n1811\t\t}\n1812\t\n1813\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1814\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1815\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1816\t\t}\n1817\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1818\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1819\t\t}\n1820\t\n1821\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1822\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1823\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1824\t\n1825\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1826\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1827\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1828\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1829\t\t\t} else {\n1830\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1831\t\t\t}\n1832\t\t}\n1833\t\n1834\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1835\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1836\t\n1837\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1838\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1839\t\n1840\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1841\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1842\t\n1843\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1844\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1845\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1846\t\n1847\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1848\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1850\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1851\t\t}\n1852\t\n1853\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1854\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1855\t\n1856\t\t// Add field name if present\n1857\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1858\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1859\t\t}\n1860\t\n1861\t\t// Add message\n1862\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1865\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1866\t\n1867\t\treturn builder.String()\n1868\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1871\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1872\t\tswitch category {\n1873\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1874\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1875\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1876\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1877\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1878\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1879\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1880\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1881\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1882\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1883\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1884\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1885\t\tdefault:\n1886\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1887\t\t}\n1888\t}\n1889\t\n1890\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1891\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1892\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n1893\t\tif config == nil {\n1894\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1895\t\t}\n1896\t\n1897\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1898\t\n1899\t\tswitch category {\n1900\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1901\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1902\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1903\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1904\t\t\t}\n1905\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1906\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1907\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1908\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n1909\t\t\t}\n1910\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1911\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n1912\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n1913\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n1914\t\t\t}\n1915\t\t}\n1916\t\n1917\t\treturn suffix.String()\n1918\t}\n1919\t\n1920\t// =============================================================================\n1921\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n1922\t// =============================================================================\n1923\t\n1924\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n1925\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n1926\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n1927\t//\n1928\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n1929\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. It handles the most common validation\n1930\t// error scenarios with a clean, easy-to-use API.\n1931\t//\n1932\t// # Parameters\n1933\t//\n1934\t//   - errorType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n1935\t//     Required. This identifies what kind of validation was performed.\n1936\t//\n1937\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n1938\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n1939\t//\n1940\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type)\n1941\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n1942\t//\n1943\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n1944\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n1945\t//\n1946\t// # Returns\n1947\t//\n1948\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n1949\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n1950\t//\n1951\t// # Features\n1952\t//\n1953\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n1954\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n1955\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n1956\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n1957\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n1958\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n1959\t//\n1960\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n1961\t//\n1962\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n1963\t//\n1964\t//\terr := - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n1991\t//   - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n1992\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n1993\t//   - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n1994\t//   - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n1995\t//   - Custom types: Any string works for Example usage:\n24\t//\n25\t//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n26\t//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n27\t//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\n28\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n29\t\tif resp == nil {\n30\t\t\treturn false\n31\t\t}\n32\t\n33\t\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n34\t\n35\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n36\t\tcase int:\n37\t\t\treturn statusCode == v\n38\t\tcase []int:\n39\t\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n40\t\t\t\tif statusCode == code {\n41\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n42\t\t\t\t}\n43\t\t\t}\n44\t\t\treturn false\n45\t\tdefault:\n46\t\t\treturn false\n47\t\t}\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n51\t//\n52\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n53\t//\n54\t// Example usage:\n55\t//\n56\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsError(response) {\n57\t//\t    // Handle error response\n58\t//\t}\n59\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n60\t\tif resp == nil {\n61\t\t\treturn false\n62\t\t}\n63\t\n64\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n68\t//\n69\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n70\t//\n71\t// Example usage:\n72\t//\n73\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n74\t//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n75\t//\t}\n76\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n77\t\tif resp == nil {\n78\t\t\treturn `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n    Suggestions        []string      `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Error Formatting Patterns**\n\n**Builder Pattern vs 5xx)\n- Validation context\n\n### 5. **Helper Functions and Utilities**\n\n**Field Reference Formatting:**\n- `FormatFieldRef()`: Standardized field name formatting with array index support\n- indexing\n\n**Convenience Functions:**\n- `FormatStatusCodeError()`: HTTP status code errors\n- `FormatErrorMessageError()`: Error message pattern errors\n- `FormatStatusCodeRangeError()`: Status code Here's what I found:\n\n### 1. **Error Definition and Handling Structure**\n\n**Core Error Types:**\n- **ValidationError** (`error_types.go`): The main error struct with comprehensive fields\n- **ValidationErrorData** (`error_types.go`): Simplified, serialization-friendly version\n- **ErrorCategory** (`error_categories.go`): High-level categorization (HTTP, Content, Validation, Performance, Security, Custom)\n- **ErrorSeverity** (`error_categorization.go`): Severity levels (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info)\n\n**Error Type Enumerations:**\n- **ValidationErrorType** (`error_type_enum.go`): HTTP/API specific types (status_code, error_message, content_type, etc.)\n- **ErrorType** (`error_type.go`): Generic validation types (required, format, range, length, type, value, duplicate, conflict)\n\n### 2. **Error Types and Structures**\n\n**ValidationError Structure:**\n```go\ntype ValidationError struct {\n    // Required fields\n    ErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n    Message   string `json:\"message\"`\n    \n    // Optional fields\n    Context            string        `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n    Expected           interface{}   `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n    Actual             interface{}   `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n    FieldName          string        `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n    Location           string        `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n    RelatedFields      []string      `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n    PatternDetails     string        `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n    RangeInfo          string        `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n    ValidationDetails  []string      `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n    ResponseSnippet    string        `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n    Suggestions        []string      `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Error Formatting Patterns**\n\n**Builder Pattern (`format_helper.go`):**\n- **ValidationFormatter**: Fluent API for constructing errors\n- Method chaining: `NewValidationFormatter().WithExpected().WithActual().WithContext().Format()`\n\n**Format Options System:**\n```go\ntype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n// WithContext, WithResponseSnippet, WithFieldName, WithSuggestions, etc.\n```\n\n**Key Formatting Functions:**\n- `FormatValidationErrorHelper()`: Main entry point for validation errors\n- `FormatErrorWithSeverity()`: Severity-aware formatting\n- `FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity()`: Category and severity aware\n- Category-specific functions: `FormatHTTPError()`, `FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity()`, etc.\n\n### 4. **Error Message Patterns**\n\n**Standard Error Format Structure:**\n```\n{error_type} validation failed\n  Expected: {expected_value}\n  Actual:   {actual_value}\n  Context:  {context}\n  Field:    {field_name}\n  Pattern:  {pattern_details}\n  Range:    {range_info}\n  Response: {response_snippet}\n  Details:\n    - {detail_1}\n    - {detail_2}\n  Suggestions:\n    - {suggestion_1}\n    - {suggestion_2}\n```\n\n**Auto-generated Suggestions:**\nThe system includes intelligent suggestion generation based on:\n- Error type (status_code, error_message, content_type, etc.)\n- Actual values received (404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n- HTTP status code categories (4xx vs 5xx)\n- Validation context\n\n### 5. **Helper Functions and Utilities**\n\n**Field Reference Formatting:**\n- `FormatFieldRef()`: Standardized field name formatting with array index support\n- `FormatFieldPath()`: Path normalization (converts \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n- Quote style support: NoQuote, SingleQuote, DoubleQuote, Backtick\n\n**Error Grouping and Indexing:**\n- **ErrorFieldGroup**: Group errors by field name\n- **ErrorCategoryGroup**: Group errors by category\n- **ErrorSeverityGroup**: Group errors by severity\n- **ErrorIndex**: Multi-dimensional error indexing\n\n**Convenience Functions:**\n- `FormatStatusCodeError()`: HTTP status code errors\n- `FormatErrorMessageError()`: Error message pattern errors\n- `FormatStatusCodeRangeError()`: Status code range errors\n- `FormatContentTypeError()`: Content-Type header errors\n\n### 6. **Key Design Patterns**\n\n**1. Builder Pattern:**\n```go\nerr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n**2. Functional Options Pattern:**\n```go\nerr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n    \"custom_type\", expected, actual,\n    WithContext(\"operation\"),\n    WithSuggestions(\"fix 1\", \"fix 2\"),\n)\n```\n\n**3. Error Type Enums:**\n- Type-safe error classification\n- String representations for serialization\n- Validation and helper methods\n\n### 7. **Advanced Features**\n\n**Severity System:**\n- 5-level severity hierarchy\n- Visual indicators (🚨, ⚠️, ⚡, ℹ️, 💡)\n- Severity-based formatting\n\n**Category System:**\n- 6 main categories (HTTP, Content, Validation, Performance, Security, Custom)\n- Category-aware formatting\n- Category-specific context\n\n**Error Tracking:**\n- Invalid error type tracking for debugging\n- Thread-safe error monitoring\n- Configuration validation\n\n### 8. **Example Usage Patterns**\n\n**Basic Usage:**\n```go\nerr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\nlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n```\n\n**Advanced Usage:**\n```go\nerr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"error_message\",\n    \"invalid.*token\",\n    \"access_denied\",\n    validate.WithContext(\"OAuth validation\"),\n    validate.WithFieldName(\"error\"),\n    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check token validity\", \"Refresh token\"))\n```\n\n**Category-Aware Usage:**\n```go\nerr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n    \"Field is required\",\n    \"email\",\n    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n)\n```\n\n### Summary\n\nThe ARMOR validation error system is a production-ready, comprehensive framework that provides:\n\n1. **Consistent Structure**: Single ValidationError type for all validation failures\n2.
